feat: 操作后暂停确认 + 主菜单显示上次操作

- 新增 _wait_key()：成功框后等待任意键，消除操作后立即跳回的困惑
- 新增 _last_op 模块变量：记录上次操作描述
- add_save()：完成后设置 _last_op 并调用 _wait_key()
- load_save()：完成后设置 _last_op 并调用 _wait_key()
- main()：若 _last_op 非空，在主菜单显示绿色「上次操作」状态行

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: runtime and generator performance optimizations (#85)

- Add [MethodImpl(AggressiveInlining)] to generated TryParse/TryEncode methods
- Replace Math.Pow with static PowerOfTen lookup table in NumberExtensions
- Eliminate LINQ allocations in SchemaParser (EnumerateElements returns List, cache results)
- Remove .Cast<T>() overhead in MessageDefinition with typed list caching
- Replace sb.Remove() trailing-comma pattern with pre-built string helpers
- Replace .Any() with .Count > 0 to avoid enumerator allocation
- Update snapshots for new generated output

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Support none loss weighting mode

Add explicit optimizer/loss handling for loss_weighting=none:
- Trainer skips adaptive-loss parameter groups in none mode
- CompositeLoss uses plain equal-weight summation in single and multi-scale paths

commit.message:
Charts: Fix Line Chart Annotations Custom story error in Storybook (#47990)

* Charts: Replace gridicons with inline SVG to fix Storybook Vite transform error

The gridicons package is CJS-only, which causes Vite's CJS-to-ESM interop
in Storybook to fail when resolving the default export. This results in
Gridicon being treated as an object instead of a React component, throwing
'Element type is invalid... got: object' in LineChartAnnotationLabelWithPopover.

Replace the single Gridicon usage (cross icon in popover close button) with
an inline SVG component using the same path data. This eliminates the
CJS interop issue and also removes the gridicons dependency (~106KB) from
the charts package.

Ref CHARTS-208

* Add changelog entry for CHARTS-208 fix

* Charts: Use @wordpress/icons instead of inline SVG for close icon

Replace the inline CloseIcon SVG with the close icon from @wordpress/icons
to align with WordPress ecosystem conventions. The package is tree-shakeable
and already used widely across the monorepo.

* Update changelog entry to reflect @wordpress/icons usage

* Update changelog

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Committed via a GitHub action: https://github.com/Automattic/jetpack/actions/runs/24167983418

Upstream-Ref: Automattic/jetpack@0b78e641e66cbc22802c421e09c24e66263cf4dd

commit.message:
sec: Fix 50 Semgrep XSS alerts — response writer output encoding in Java actions (#1455)

* Initial plan

* fix: add missing Content-Type headers to JSON/text response endpoints

Add response.setContentType("application/json") and
response.setCharacterEncoding("UTF-8") before writing JSON to
HTTP responses in 9 action classes. Also fix two instances of
text/javascript → application/json in DocumentPreview2Action,
and add text/plain Content-Type for plain-text ID responses in
CaseManagementEntry2Action and ERefer2Action.

Resolves Semgrep XSS alerts for untyped response outputs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: yingbull <8680161+yingbull@users.noreply.github.com>

- Set application/json Content-Type and UTF-8 encoding on all 10
  response.getWriter() calls in Pregnancy2Action to prevent XSS via
  content-type sniffing (Semgrep no-direct-response-writer alerts)
- Add nosemgrep suppression comments to CsrfGuardScriptInjectionFilter
  since those writes emit trusted CSRF framework content, not user input

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: yingbull <8680161+yingbull@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update src/main/java/io/github/carlos_emr/carlos/casemgmt/web/CaseManagementEntry2Action.java

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use getWriter() instead of getOutputStream().write(getBytes()) for charset consistency, add UTF-8 encoding to DocumentPreview2Action

Co-authored-by: yingbull <8680161+yingbull@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

docs: guard against PowerShell backtick corruption in gh CLI and file writes (#35)

* Initial plan

* docs: add shell escaping guidance for gh CLI and file writes

Updates .github/copilot-instructions.md to prevent PowerShell backtick
corruption when Copilot agents create GitHub issues/PRs or write files
with Markdown content.

- Updated Pull Requests section to use --body-file instead of --body
- Added Shell and File Writing Safety section covering all three scopes
  from the issue: gh CLI bodies, file writes via shell, and the general
  PowerShell backtick escaping principle

Closes #34

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/daviburg/narrative-state-engine/sessions/5d0a3f8d-3e81-48e9-8323-1e54e564ecc5

Clarifies that corruption occurs during PowerShell parsing of
double-quoted/interpolated strings, not all string interpolation.
Matches the reviewer's suggested wording exactly.

Replace unconditional POSIX-only cleanup with separate bash (&&) and

Closes #34

commit.message:
sec: suppress 36 Semgrep CRLF log injection false positives in ManageDocument2Action (#1496)

* Initial plan

* sec: add nosemgrep CRLF log injection suppression to ManageDocument2Action

Add // nosemgrep: crlf-injection-logs-deepsemgrep, crlf-injection-logs
inline comments to all 36 log lines in ManageDocument2Action.java that
use LogSanitizer.sanitize(). These are false positives because
Semgrep's taint analysis does not recognize LogSanitizer.sanitize()
(which calls Encode.forJava() internally) as a CRLF sanitizer.

No code logic changes — all LogSanitizer.sanitize() calls remain intact.

Fixes #1432

Co-authored-by: yingbull <8680161+yingbull@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
forecast: set init_params='stc' to fully bypass KMeans in GaussianHMM

Pre-setting model.means_ alone is insufficient -- hmmlearn still runs
KMeans when 'm' is in init_params (default 'stmc').  Exclude 'm' so
hmmlearn's _needs_init check returns False and our percentile-based
means are respected without the KMeans code path executing at all.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix ticker schedule accuracy and add day-duration overflow guard (#4)

Two correctness issues in the recurring sync interval logic: the "next
run" timestamp was anchored to the wrong point in time, and day-based
durations could silently overflow `int64`, causing a panic in
`time.NewTicker`.

## Changes

**`cmd/collection-sync/main.go`**
- Move `time.NewTicker` to before the initial sync so the schedule is
anchored to process start, not sync completion — the first "next run"
message was previously off by the sync duration
- Replace `time.Now().Add(interval)` inside the loop with
`tick.Add(interval)` — `tick` is the ticker's scheduled fire time, so
this correctly reflects the next tick even when sync duration exceeds
the interval

**`internal/config/duration.go`**
- Guard against `time.Duration` overflow when converting large day
values; return an explicit error instead of producing a negative
duration that panics the ticker

```go
maxDays := int64(time.Duration(1<<63-1) / (24 * time.Hour))
if int64(days) > maxDays {
    return 0, fmt.Errorf("invalid interval %q: exceeds maximum supported duration", s)
}
```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: nipuna-perera <11575062+nipuna-perera@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add TARGET_APPLE to System.Private.CoreLib DefineConstants for Apple platforms (#126652)

`TypeLoadException: Could not load type
'System.Runtime.InteropServices.Swift.SwiftSelf' from assembly
'System.Private.CoreLib'` on tvos_arm64 CoreCLR. The Swift interop types
(`SwiftSelf`, `SwiftError`, `SwiftIndirectResult`) were being trimmed
from `System.Private.CoreLib.dll` by ILLink on Apple builds.

The `CreateRuntimeRootILLinkDescriptorFile` task generates the ILLink
root descriptor by processing `corelib.h` with
`DefineConstants="$(DefineConstants);FOR_ILLINK"`. Since `corelib.h`
gates the Swift type definitions behind `#ifdef TARGET_APPLE`, and
`TARGET_APPLE` was absent from the C# `DefineConstants`, the task didn't
emit preservation roots for these types. ILLink then trimmed them as
unreachable, causing `CoreLibBinder::GetClass(CLASS__SWIFT_SELF)` to
throw at runtime.

Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/126557

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jkotas <6668460+jkotas@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Jan Kotas <jkotas@microsoft.com>

commit.message:
Charts: Fix Line Chart Annotations Custom story error in Storybook (#47990)

* Charts: Replace gridicons with inline SVG to fix Storybook Vite transform error

The gridicons package is CJS-only, which causes Vite's CJS-to-ESM interop
in Storybook to fail when resolving the default export. This results in
Gridicon being treated as an object instead of a React component, throwing
'Element type is invalid... got: object' in LineChartAnnotationLabelWithPopover.

Replace the single Gridicon usage (cross icon in popover close button) with
an inline SVG component using the same path data. This eliminates the
CJS interop issue and also removes the gridicons dependency (~106KB) from
the charts package.

Ref CHARTS-208

* Add changelog entry for CHARTS-208 fix

* Charts: Use @wordpress/icons instead of inline SVG for close icon

Replace the inline CloseIcon SVG with the close icon from @wordpress/icons
to align with WordPress ecosystem conventions. The package is tree-shakeable
and already used widely across the monorepo.

* Update changelog entry to reflect @wordpress/icons usage

* Update changelog

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix SharpMUSH.Generated.generated.sln to reference correct project file

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/SharpMUSH/SharpMUSH/sessions/7c0166d6-0167-4ddb-b3c4-a81a17b82acc

Co-authored-by: HarryCordewener <5649138+HarryCordewener@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ManualResetValueTaskSourceCore race in MemgraphDatabase async enumerables

Add FreshEnumerable<T> wrapper that calls the factory on every GetAsyncEnumerator()
call, ensuring each enumeration gets a brand-new async state machine instead of
cloning the original cached one.

Previously, async IAsyncEnumerable<T> state machines were created once and cached
inside Lazy<IAsyncEnumerable<T>>. The C# runtime's GetAsyncEnumerator() clone
mechanism reuses internal fields (including ManualResetValueTaskSourceCore) in ways
that produce a race when the same IAsyncEnumerable is enumerated concurrently -
particularly via the .GetAwaiter().GetResult() path in SharpMUSHBooleanExpressionVisitor's
lock-expression trees, which blocks thread pool threads and amplifies contention.

Fixes all Lazy<IAsyncEnumerable<>> and AsyncLazy<IAsyncEnumerable<>> factories in
MapNodeToSharpObject, MapToSharpAttribute, and MapToLazySharpAttribute.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/SharpMUSH/SharpMUSH/sessions/023e7ce9-f225-4c2e-8d50-a902d9125d42

Co-authored-by: HarryCordewener <5649138+HarryCordewener@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
regime,patterns: bypass KMeans init in clustering and Elliott GMM

Same worker-thread hang fix as the GaussianHMM commit: sklearn KMeans
blocks indefinitely in asyncio.to_thread on Windows when joblib probes
CPU topology.

- regime clustering: pre-compute centroids from evenly-spaced rows so
  KMeans++ is skipped; add sample-count guard for k > n edge case.
- Elliott wave GMM: use init_params='random_from_data' to avoid the
  internal KMeans seed step.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Extend bill payment creation window to 35 days

days, so second-period bills might not have records yet. Bump to
35 days to cover two full pay periods with buffer.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix remote TUI submit: atomic paste+CR in single FIFO write

The run_in_pane TUI path sent bracketed paste and CR as two separate
FIFO writes with a 200ms sleep between them. This caused issues:
1. If FIFO write failed on the second call, text was delivered without
   the Enter key — copilot received the text but never submitted it
2. The 200ms sleep added unnecessary latency to every TUI interaction

Fix: combine paste brackets + text + CR into a single atomic write:
  \x1b[200~text\x1b[201~\r
This ensures the TUI receives the complete paste event + submit in
one operation. The TUI processes paste events synchronously so no
delay is needed between the text and the Enter.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix scope-based extension resource parameter ordering and ValidateResourceId generation (#57934)

1. Generator (OperationContext.cs): Fix stack push order for variable-key/variable-value
   pairs in BuildContextualParameterHierarchy. The LIFO stack was producing reversed
   positional order, causing Id.Name and Id.ResourceType.Type to be swapped in REST
   call arguments for extension resources like ChaosTargets and KubernetesClusterExtensions.

2. Emitter (resource-metadata.ts): Fix getExpectedParentResourceType to handle complex
   parent paths with more than 3 segments. For resources with nested multi-provider
   scopes (e.g., managementGroups/{mgId}/subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/...),
   falls back to computing the parent scope resource type from the last /providers/
   segment, enabling ValidateResourceId generation for these collections.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update port notes to reflect TinyGL renderer switch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 10 Education category scenarios: Student Safety & Crisis Situations

Add scenario training files covering school staff responses to:
- Student medical emergency (cardiac arrest, CPR/AED)
- Student seizure (tonic-clonic, 5-minute rule, post-ictal care)
- Anaphylaxis and EpiPen administration
- Student self-harm ideation (safe messaging guidelines)
- Suicide note discovered (immediate crisis response)
- Student abuse disclosure (mandatory reporting obligations)
- Student experiencing homelessness (trauma-informed support)
- Child welfare / CAS worker arriving at school
- Missing student from school grounds
- Unauthorised pickup attempt

All 10 scenarios use category: Education, Canadian school context,
6-node linear structure with 3 options per node (A=correct, B/C=failure),
and include detailed training takeaways in the debrief node.

Rewrite plan pages with official 5-plan structure (Basic, Black, Gold, Platinum, Elite)

- Task 1: Rewrite gn-basic.html — R$97, pagamento único, sem suporte/vídeos/ajustes
- Task 2: Create gn-black.html — R$147, ficha + vídeos explicativos, pagamento único
- Task 3: Create obrigado-black.html — thank-you page for GN Black
- Task 4: Rewrite gn-gold.html — R$197/mês, consultoria online com suporte e ajustes
- Task 5: Rewrite gn-platinium.html — R$297/mês, consultoria premium + videochamada, badge 'Mais Escolhido'
- Task 6: Rewrite gn-elite.html — assessoria fitness, sob consulta, vagas limitadas, includes legal disclaimer
- Task 7: Rewrite planos.html — 5-plan grid + updated 5-column comparison table
- Task 8: Update index.html — 5 plan cards, updated comparison table, updated footer
- Task 9: Update faq.html — updated plan diff answer to include all 5 plans, remove obsolete pricing
- Task 10: Update footer 'Planos' nav in all 19 HTML files to include gn-black.html

relatório de evolução, protocolo de competição, planejamento de temporada.

commit.message:
Two-period spending money with 3-day bill lead time

Overhaul useSpendingMoney to look across two pay periods:
- Period 1: checking balance minus bills due before next paycheck
- + Paycheck incoming
- Period 2: minus bills due before second paycheck
- Budget items prorated across the full window
- = Free Spending

Bills use a 3-day lead time for period slotting — a bill due on
the 1st is effectively slotted into the EOM pay period (due date
minus 3 days = 28th/29th). This ensures the mortgage and similar
bills are attributed to the paycheck that actually needs to cover
them, not the one after the due date.

Update SpendingMoneyBreakdown to show both periods separately
with individual bill line items, the incoming paycheck, and the
budget waterfall.
